title: Just So
tagline: Some of Rudyard Kipling's best stories brought to the stage!
synopsis: An adaptation of Rudyard Kipling's classic Just So Stories, exploring how various animals acquired their distinctive features — the kangaroo's legs, the cat's solitary nature, the rhino's itchy skin, and the elephant's trunk.
source_material: Just So Stories by Rudyard Kipling
cast_notes: 25 speaking roles plus flexible chorus; most roles are gender-neutral
licensing_includes: Backing tracks for rehearsal and performance
premiere_venue: Open Door Repertory Company, Oak Park, Illinois

title: Becky Thatcher
tagline: A new look at Mark Twain's Hannibal, MO — this time focusing on Becky instead of Tom!
synopsis: A stage adaptation of Mark Twain's classic, reimagined from Becky's perspective. Features familiar adventures like the whitewash scene while introducing audiences to the girls of Hannibal, Missouri. Tom Sawyer with none of the controversy.
source_material: The Adventures of Tom Sawyer by Mark Twain
cast_notes: 25 speaking roles plus flexible chorus. More female roles than male; gender can be switched for many parts.
licensing_notes: Unlimited performances within a 2-week calendar period
licensing_includes: Backing tracks for rehearsal and performance

title: Off to Olympus
tagline: A rollicking re-imagining of some of the legends in Greek Mythology.

title: Pots and Pan
tagline: The next chapter in the story of the boy who refuses to grow up — Peter Pan!

title: Tut, Tut!
tagline: King Tut meets the Prince and the Pauper.
source_material: The Prince and the Pauper / Ancient Egypt
licensing_notes: Published by Beat by Beat Press

title: Bounce!
tagline: A 30-minute musical about Reggie, a 10-year-old with endless energy.
licensing_notes: Published by Beat by Beat Press

title: The Show Must Go Online
tagline: The world's first virtual children's musical — 1,000+ productions in 40 countries.
synopsis: A virtual children's musical written in 19 days during the COVID-19 pandemic. Became the most-licensed musical of 2020.
licensing_notes: Published by Beat by Beat Press. Most-licensed musical of 2020.
